key_points: Democracy is a form of government in which the rulers are elected by the people
The word democracy comes from the Greek words 'demos' meaning people and 'kratia' meaning rule
The basic idea of democracy is rule by the people
In a democracy, the final decision-making power must rest with leaders elected by the people
Free and fair elections are essential for a democratic government
The principle 'one person, one vote, one value' ensures political equality
Universal adult franchise gives every adult citizen the right to vote
Democratic governments must respect citizens' rights and follow the constitution
Democracy encourages discussion and consultation before decisions are made
Democracy allows peaceful change of rulers through elections
examples: Military influence example: Pakistan under General Musharraf
Largest democracy in the world: India
Example of democratic value: Freedom of expression
Example of citizen participation: Voting in elections
formulas: Democracy = Government elected by the people
Political equality = One person one vote one value
Universal adult franchise = Every adult citizen has the right to vote
Representative democracy = People elect representatives to make decisions on their behalf
exam_tips: Remember the four key features of democracy: elected rulers, free elections, political equality and rule of law
Understand the examples of Pakistan, China and Mexico used to explain democracy
Focus on advantages of democracy such as better decision making and peaceful conflict resolution
Democracy enhances the dignity of citizens by treating them as equals
Democracy allows correction of mistakes through elections
